title: Furniture makers and other craft woodworkers
description: Create or reproduce handmade objects for sale and exhibition using a variety of techniques, such as welding, weaving, pottery, and needlecraft. Construct, erect, install, or repair structures and fixtures made of wood and comparable materials, such as concrete forms; building frameworks, including partitions, joists, studding, and rafters; and wood stairways, window and door frames, and hardwood floors. May also install cabinets, siding, drywall, and batt or roll insulation. Includes brattice builders who build doors or brattices (ventilation walls or partitions) in underground passageways. Assemble, install, or maintain solar photovoltaic (PV) systems on roofs or other structures in compliance with site assessment and schematics. May include measuring, cutting, assembling, and bolting structural framing and solar modules. May perform minor electrical work such as current checks. (Based on 21 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Trim wood parts according to specifications, using planes, chisels, or wood files or sanders. Examine raw woodstock for defects and to ensure conformity to size and other specification standards. Shape or cut materials to specified measurements, using hand tools, machines, or power saws. Inspect and mark completed workpieces and stack them on pallets, in boxes, or on conveyors so that they can be moved to the next workstation. Examine finished workpieces for smoothness, shape, angle, depth-of-cut, or conformity to specifications and verify dimensions, visually and using hands, rules, calipers, templates, or gauges. Measure and mark cutting lines on materials, using a ruler, pencil, chalk, and marking gauge.

Grease or oil woodworking machines. Clean or maintain products, machines, or work areas. Maintain job records and schedule work crew. Follow established safety rules and regulations and maintain a safe and clean environment. Remove damaged or defective parts or sections of structures and repair or replace, using hand tools. Inspect pulleys, drive belts, guards, or fences on machines to ensure that machines will operate safely.

Start machines and move levers to engage hydraulic lifts that press woodstocks into desired forms and disengage lifts after appropriate drying times. Secure woodstock against a guide or in a holding device, place woodstock on a conveyor, or dump woodstock in a hopper to feed woodstock into machines. Feed stock through feed mechanisms or conveyors into planing, shaping, boring, mortising, or sanding machines to produce desired components. Start machines, adjust controls, and make trial cuts to ensure that machinery is operating properly. Monitor operation of machines and make adjustments to correct problems and ensure conformance to specifications.

Operate gluing machines to glue pieces of wood together, or to press and affix wood veneer to wood surfaces. Assemble and fasten materials to make frameworks or props, using hand tools and wood screws, nails, dowel pins, or glue. Install structures or fixtures, such as windows, frames, floorings, trim, or hardware, using carpenters' hand or power tools. Assemble gates, and fasten gates into position, using hand tools. Construct forms or chutes for pouring concrete. Build or repair cabinets, doors, frameworks, floors, or other wooden fixtures used in buildings, using woodworking machines, carpenter's hand tools, or power tools.

Set up, program, operate, or tend computerized or manual woodworking machines, such as drill presses, lathes, shapers, routers, sanders, planers, or wood-nailing machines. Attach and adjust guides, stops, clamps, chucks, or feed mechanisms, using hand tools. Change alignment and adjustment of sanding, cutting, or boring machine guides to prevent defects in finished products, using hand tools. Select knives, saws, blades, cutter heads, cams, bits, or belts, according to workpiece, machine functions, or product specifications. Adjust machine tables or cutting devices and set controls on machines to produce specified cuts or operations. Equip and maintain tools and equipment, ensuring functionality and safety standards.

Inspect ceiling or floor tile, wall coverings, siding, glass, or woodwork to detect broken or damaged structures. Examine structural timbers and supports to detect decay, and replace timbers as required, using hand tools. Adjust string tensions to tune instruments, using hand tools and electronic tuning devices. Install and adjust blades, cutterheads, boring-bits, or sanding-belts, using hand tools and rules. Verify trueness of structure, using a plumb bob and level. Connect water hoses to inlet pipes of plumbing systems, and test operation of plumbing fixtures.

Dig or direct digging of post holes and set poles to support structures. Erect scaffolding or ladders for assembling structures above ground level. Inspect, examine, and test the operation of parts or systems to evaluate operating condition and to determine if repairs are needed. Repair leaks in plumbing or gas lines, using caulking compounds and plastic or copper pipe. Move and set up mobile homes or prefabricated buildings on owners' lots or at mobile home parks. Speak with customers to discuss furniture or construction needs and estimate costs accordingly.

Perform minor plumbing, welding, or concrete mixing work. Explain energy conservation measures, such as the use of low flow showerheads and energy-efficient lighting. Apply weather-stripping, glazing, caulking, or door sweeps to reduce energy losses. Cut and shape edges of wood or structures to ensure compatibility for assembly or installation projects. Remove or limit contamination following emergencies involving hazardous substances. Prepare hazardous material for removal or storage.

Operate cranes to move or load baskets, casks, or canisters. Arrange for subcontractors to deal with special areas, such as heating or electrical wiring work. Locate and repair frayed wiring, broken connections, or incorrect wiring, using ohmmeters, soldering irons, tape, and hand tools. Identify or separate waste products or materials for recycling or reuse. Deliver pianos to purchasers or to locations of their use. Apply bioremediation techniques to hazardous wastes to allow naturally occurring bacteria to break down toxic substances.

Control hoists to remove parts or products from work stations. Unclamp workpieces and remove them from machines. Examine and reassemble musical instruments or components for assessment, repairs, and adjustments. Polish instruments, using rags and polishing compounds, buffing wheels, or burnishing tools. Refinish and polish piano cabinets or cases to prepare them for sale. Make wood replacement parts, using woodworking machines and hand tools.

Weld metal parts together, using portable gas welding equipment. Cover subfloors with building paper to keep out moisture and lay hardwood, parquet, or wood-strip-block floors by nailing floors to subfloor or cementing them to mastic or asphalt base. Apply insulation materials, such as loose, blanket, board, and foam insulation to attics, crawl spaces, basements, or walls. Adjust felt hammers on pianos to increase tonal mellowness or brilliance, using sanding paddles, lacquer, or needles. Arrange for packaging and storage of hazardous materials or related waste.
Comply with prescribed safety procedures or federal laws regulating waste disposal methods.
Draw up detailed specifications and discuss projects with customers.
Fill and smooth cracks or depressions, remove marks and imperfections, and repair broken parts using plastic or wood putty, glue, nails, or screws.
Fit, fasten, and assemble wood parts together to form patterns, models, or sections, using glue, nails, dowels, bolts, screws, and other fasteners.

Brush, spray, or hand-rub finishing ingredients such as paint, oil, stain, or wax onto and into wood grain and apply lacquer or other sealers, finishing patterns or models with protective or decorative coatings.
Smooth, shape, and touch up surfaces to prepare them for finishing, using sandpaper, pumice stones, steel wool, chisels, sanders, or grinders.

Select appropriate finishing ingredients such as paint, stain, lacquer, shellac, or varnish depending on factors such as wood hardness and surface type, and mix finish ingredients to obtain desired colors or shades.

Examine furniture to determine the extent of damage or deterioration and decide on the best method for repair or restoration, treating warped or stained surfaces to restore original contours and colors.
Verify dimensions and contours of models during hand-forming processes using templates and measuring devices, and compute dimensions, areas, volumes, and weights.

Construct wooden models, patterns, templates, full-scale mock-ups, and molds for parts of products and production tools, selecting wooden stock and determining layouts and marking layouts of parts on stock.

Read blueprints, drawings, or written specifications, and consult with designers to determine sizes and shapes of patterns and required machine setups, as well as establish the specifications of articles to be constructed or repaired.
Set up, operate, and adjust a variety of woodworking machines such as bandsaws and planers to cut and shape sections, parts, and patterns, according to specifications.
Examine and measure completed materials or products to verify conformance to specifications using measuring devices, and inspect workpieces to determine work procedures and materials required.

Apply coatings such as paint, ink, or lacquer to protect or decorate workpiece surfaces, and examine finished surfaces of workpieces to verify conformance to specifications and retouch any defective areas.

Disassemble items to prepare them for finishing using hand tools, and remove old finishes and damaged or deteriorated parts using hand tools, stripping tools, sandpaper, steel wool, abrasives, solvents, or dip baths.
Maintain pattern records for reference, and inventory equipment and supplies, ordering parts and tools as necessary.
Transport materials and products to and from work areas manually or using carts, hand trucks, or hoists.
Demonstrate system functionality and performance, including start-up, shut-down, normal operation, and emergency or bypass operations.
Perform routine maintenance on tools and equipment, using solvents, brushes, and rags.

Identify and resolve any deficiencies in photovoltaic (PV) system installation or materials, and determine photovoltaic (PV) system designs or configurations based on factors such as customer needs, expectations, and site conditions.
Install hardware such as hinges, handles, catches, or drawer pulls, using hand tools.
Repair or alter wooden furniture, cabinetry, fixtures, paneling, or other pieces.
Adjust machine components according to specifications for widths, lengths, and thicknesses of materials and amounts of glue, cement, or adhesive required.
Load material into feeding mechanisms of machines and monitor machine operations to detect malfunctions and report or resolve problems.
Select and mix ingredients to prepare coating substances according to specifications.

Inspect materials, products, or equipment to detect defects or malfunctions, and ensure conformity to production standards. Plan and establish work schedules, assignments, and production sequences to meet production goals.

Confer with customers to assess needs or obtain feedback on crafted products. Plan and attend craft shows to market products, and advertise using various media such as internet advertising and brochures.

Develop concepts or creative ideas for craft objects, and sketch or draw objects to be crafted. Create functional or decorative objects by hand, using a variety of methods and materials, and apply finishes to objects being crafted.

Select materials for crafting based on characteristics such as strength, color, and texture, and set specifications for materials, dimensions, and finishes. Fabricate patterns or templates to guide craft production and create prototypes or models of objects.

Cut, shape, fit, join, mold, or process materials using hand tools, power tools, or machinery. Monitor tool and part inventories and maintain working conditions in shops to ensure equipment is in good repair.

Conduct or arrange worker training in safety, repair, or maintenance techniques, operational procedures, or equipment use. Enforce safety regulations and provide employee safety training to prevent unsafe conditions or violations.
Calculate labor and equipment requirements, compute estimates and actual costs of factors such as materials and labor, and maintain records of employees' attendance and hours worked.

Examine objects, systems, or facilities and analyze information to determine needed installations, services, or repairs. Perform skilled repair or maintenance operations using hand or power tools, or other equipment.
Develop, implement, or evaluate maintenance policies and procedures. Monitor employees' work levels and review work performance.

Research craft trends, venues, and customer buying patterns to inspire designs and marketing strategies. Develop designs using specialized computer software, or use computer software to design patterns for engraving.

Inspect etched work for depth, uniformity, and defects using calibrated tools, and adjust machine settings and depths or sizes of cuts during production. Investigate accidents or injuries and prepare reports of findings.

Prepare workpieces for etching by cutting, sanding, and treating them with various materials. Transfer images to workpieces using various devices, and engrave and print patterns onto a variety of surfaces using tools.
Remove imperfections or prepare finished workpieces using specialized techniques such as neutralizing or sandblasting.
Use machinery to produce work, including setting reduction scales, inserting cutting tools, and ensuring correct machine settings for production.
Remove completed workpieces and pack products for shipping, ensuring compliance with quality standards.
Compile operational or personnel records, such as time and production records, and recommend measures to improve production methods and product quality.
Direct and coordinate the activities of employees engaged in production or processing of goods, and confer with other supervisors to coordinate operations and activities.
Inspect, test, and measure completed work to verify standards of quality; monitor operators and gauge performance during production.
